# Copilot Instructions for Resume Helper

<!-- Use this file to provide workspace-specific custom instructions to Copilot. For more details, visit https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/copilot-customization#_use-a-githubcopilotinstructionsmd-file -->

## Project Overview
This is a Resume Helper application built with Next.js 15, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. The application helps users create, edit, and manage their resumes with a modern UI and export capabilities.

## Key Technologies
- **Next.js 15** with App Router
- **TypeScript** for type safety
- **Tailwind CSS** for styling
- **React** for UI components

## Development Guidelines
1. Use **TypeScript** for all new files
2. Follow **Next.js App Router** conventions
3. Use **Tailwind CSS** for styling - prefer utility classes
4. Create reusable components in `src/components/`
5. Use **React hooks** and modern patterns
6. Implement responsive design for mobile and desktop
7. Follow accessibility best practices
8. Use proper semantic HTML

## Code Style Preferences
- Use **arrow functions** for React components
- Prefer **const** over let/var
- Use **interface** instead of type for object shapes
- Keep components small and focused
- Use descriptive variable and function names
- Add proper TypeScript types for all props and state

## Resume Helper Specific Features
- Resume creation and editing
- Multiple resume templates
- PDF export functionality
- Real-time preview
- Section management (experience, education, skills, etc.)
- Modern, professional UI design
- Responsive layout for all devices
